While upgrading some legacy projects recently, I hit this warning: "Deprecation Warning [import]: Sass @import rules are deprecated and will be removed in Dart Sass 3.0.0." So this post is a quick note on what changed and how to migrate over to @use and @forward.

The previous post covered `@import`. This one starts pulling in earlier topics, building up to `mixin`. What's a mixin? I think of it as an SCSS library — a place to store frequently used SCSS snippets.
SCSS provides `@import`, which makes development management way easier when used well.
Operators? Like add, subtract, multiply, divide? Yep — SCSS supports operators: add (+), subtract (-), multiply (*), and divide (/).
From this post on we won't talk about SASS — let's focus on the SCSS basics.
First, big thanks to Hexschool's principal Liao Wei-Chieh for writing "30 Days to Master SASS Syntax", which I keep coming back to as I learn SCSS — it's a real godsend.
